title: Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 13th February 2026 | TV5 Money

recommendations.note: No buy now; wait for stability after the first big IT fall
recommendations.detail: Said there is no need to buy Persistent (or Coforge) now. The first major fall was just yesterday and IT is down 3-15% YTD. Have patience and wait for stability; no hurry.

recommendations.note: No buy now; wait for stability after the first big IT fall
recommendations.detail: Grouped with Persistent. No need to buy at present; let some stability come first given the sharp IT-sector correction. No hurry to enter.

recommendations.note: Exit on rallies; relief rallies not holding
recommendations.detail: Recently controversial with news flow; even after clarifications the relief rallies are not holding up. Advised exiting on rallies. (Caller was in a 20% loss.)

recommendations.note: Hold with a trailing stop
recommendations.detail: Advised holding CG Power with a trailing stop in place. (Caller was in a 20% profit.)

recommendations.note: Book ~50% profit; make the balance free of cost
recommendations.detail: Stock rose about 50% off the December low. Advised booking 50% profit here and at least making the remaining position free of cost (FOC). (Caller was in 150% profit.)

recommendations.note: Exit on rallies; weak December result
recommendations.detail: December result was weak, below expectations, with a sharply lower bottom line. Advised exiting on rallies, even if it comes close up to 200.

recommendations.note: Hold; transformative capex stage, fundamentally strong
recommendations.detail: Company is in a transformative stage with heavy capex on new-generation products and lithium batteries; the Telangana unit is yet to be commissioned. Stock may stay weak near term with weak results, but fundamentally extremely strong, so hold; no need to sell at these levels.

recommendations.note: Hold; company doing well, dairy is seasonal
recommendations.detail: Quarterly result was slightly flat; stock stabilized around 430-440 and also dipped to 360-370. Company is doing extremely well and the product portfolio is diversifying. Dairy is seasonal and procurement-rate changes cause dips, but no need to fear. Hold.

recommendations.note: Hold with extremely long-term view
recommendations.detail: Corrected since listing, but the business is improving well. Advised holding with an extremely long-term view; no need to sell at current levels.

recommendations.note: Hold; recently listed, needs time
recommendations.detail: Recently listed and needs more time frame. As of now, hold.

recommendations.note: Hold; recently listed, needs time
recommendations.detail: Grouped with the recently listed names; needs more time frame to play out. Hold for now.

recommendations.note: Exit on rallies; very weak counter at 52-week lows
recommendations.detail: Has been advised against for a while. A very weak counter trading at 52-week lows; not a stock to buy but one to exit on rallies until a turnaround in performance comes.

recommendations.note: Dark-horse holding/implementation company; news rallies fizzle
recommendations.detail: Should be seen as a holding/implementation company. Anil Ambani group on-and-off news flow brings brief rallies that fizzle out immediately. Not a counter to pay much attention to; called it a dark-horse counter.

recommendations.note: Hold; book some profit if you availed the rights
recommendations.detail: Gave good returns post ex-rights, running to ~45-46 after the last quarter result and now stabilizing around 40. No need to panic-sell for the long term. But if you bought ahead of the rights and availed them, book some profits now.

recommendations.note: Partial exit; best of its time likely over
recommendations.detail: Results are good, but he thinks the best of its time is over and it has reached a peak. Advised a partial exit at the 115-117 levels. Business may improve after peace/stability, but outperforming returns are not expected.

recommendations.note: Buy only on declines; if held, trailing stop and hold
recommendations.detail: Shows continuous height and action and has gained some fancy, but he treats it as a highly speculative bet (flat December result yet jumped from 160-165 to 190). Buy only on declines; if already bought, put a trailing stop and hold.

recommendations.note: No fresh buying; book some profits
recommendations.detail: At these levels, no fresh buying. Advised booking some profits since lower levels would have been available.

recommendations.note: Accumulate in the 920-950 support zone
recommendations.detail: Repeatedly dips below 1,000 (965; 930-940 in early Feb). Best entry as of now is the last three-to-six-month support zone; advised accumulating around 920 to 950.

recommendations.note: Hold; flagship med-equipment company
recommendations.detail: A flagship medical-equipment company (not small) that performs well. Corrected to near its 52-week low on subdued recent results, but not a price to sell. Maybe exit on rallies once it reaches the 1,580-1,600 levels. (Caller was in ~50% loss.)

recommendations.note: Hold; don't sell, P/E ~5 and high dividend
recommendations.detail: May stay weak until the PFC merger ratio gets clarity and Government of India approval comes. Don't sell, though, because it trades at a P/E of about 5 (literally peanuts) and is a high-dividend payer. Take any decision only after total restructuring clarity. (Caller in ~50% loss.)

recommendations.note: No buy now; enter ~75-76 after a further ~15% fall
recommendations.detail: At current levels, don't buy. Already at its 52-week low with no great result, but maybe some value as a manufacturing company. Advised entering close to around the 75-76 levels, i.e. after roughly another 15% correction.

recommendations.note: Exit large-cap IT on a technical rebound (from ~10% loss)
recommendations.detail: On the Giri mail about TCS/Infosys/Wipro held at ~10% loss: a mild technical rebound is likely from the sharp two-day correction (huge cash-reserve companies, now in panic; TCS below 2,600). When it comes closer, exit, as sector fancy may fade with global developments.

recommendations.note: Exit large-cap IT on a technical rebound (from ~10% loss)
recommendations.detail: Part of the same TCS/Infosys/Wipro answer. Mild rebound expected (Infosys came below 1,300); the ~10% loss has a possibility of being covered on a technical rally, so exit when it comes closer.

recommendations.note: Exit large-cap IT on a technical rebound (from ~10% loss)
recommendations.detail: Grouped in the same large-cap IT answer. Advised exiting on the technical rebound when the position comes closer to break-even, as the fancy in the IT sector may reduce.
